page: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.95 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 320 GB SSD, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance falls into the average category for both stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. Memory performance is a notable highlight; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached read bandwidth is strong, and large-block uncached write bandwidth reaches the top tier.

The instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which assist in vector calculations and cryptography. This is reflected in the strong text generation speeds during LLM inference with small 135M models, though performance drops to average for larger 7B models. Given the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is suited for budget-conscious workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations where continuous dedicated CPU performance is not mandatory.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 8 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 320 GB local SSD. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier large-block memory write bandwidth. However, its shared CPU allocation limits multi-core scaling, placing multi-core benchmarks in the average tier. The hardware supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets, facilitating efficient vector processing. This resource profile makes the cpx42 a cost-effective option for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores.

tagline: An AMD Genoa virtual server offering 8 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB SSD storage for budget-conscious workloads.
